channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Orlando Magic
channel.description: Orlando Magic trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve …
item.description: Jake Madison: While I don't have a specific date or timeline my understanding is we are close to the end of the Pelicans head coaching search. I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve Hetzel is the choice and still think that. Jamahl Mosley also remains a possibility

item.title: Bulls to hire Stephen Mervis as senior VP of basketball operations
item.description: Shams Charania: The Chicago Bulls are finalizing a deal to hire Orlando Magic assistant GM Stephen Mervis as Senior Vice President of Basketball Operations under new EVP Bryson Graham, sources tell ESPN. Bulls will also hire Brooklyn Nets executive Acie Law as Vice President of Player Personnel.

item.title: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five …
item.description: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five seasons before he was let go earlier this month, was captured using smelling salts before Orlando’s first Play-In Tournament game against the Philadelphia 76ers last month. “It heightens your awareness,” Mosley said in April. He added it was only his second time using them.

item.title: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones …
item.description: Jason Beede: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones Basketball League is former #Magic and current Grizzlies guard Kentavious Caldwell-Pope. @OBLhoops

item.title: New Orleans has held a longstanding affinity for …
item.description: The widely presumed explanation circulating at the Chicago draft combine this week: New Orleans has held a longstanding affinity for former Magic coach Jamahl Mosley — as The People's Insider Jake Fischer first reported here in November — and is believed to be taking as much time as possible to allow for Mosley to become a full-fledged prime candidate for the post after his recent dismissal in Orlando.

item.title: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points …
item.description: Josh Dubow: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points after losing Game 6 by 20+ points. The Magic did it in 1995 Eastern Conference final, losing Game 6 123-96 at Indiana and winning Game 7 105-81 at home

item.title: The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 …
item.description: Orlando and Memphis split this season's two Europe games in Berlin in London. Berlin and Paris are scheduled to serve as host cities for the two-game tour in 2028. The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 season as its launch date for a 12-to-16 team NBA Europe circuit that would be separate from #thisleague. Wemby and the Spurs played two games against Indiana in Paris in January 2025 on the NBA's last in-season visit to France.

item.title: New Orleans Pelicans to hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach on five-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as the franchise's new head coach on a five-year contract, sources tell ESPN. Mosley accepts the Pelicans job after five seasons in Orlando where he guided the Magic to three consecutive playoff berths.

item.title: Pelicans to hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach, both sides agree to five-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as the franchise's new head coach on a five-year contract, sources tell ESPN. Mosley accepts the Pelicans job after five seasons in Orlando where he guided the Magic to three consecutive playoff berths.

item.title: Jamahl Mosley was among those the Warriors were …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Jamahl Mosley was among those the Warriors were looking at for their vacant lead assistant job, but he lands another HC opportunity with the Pelicans

item.title: He's only the second rookie to record at least …
item.description: ‪Justin Kubatko‬: Dylan Harper last night: ✅ 24 PTS ✅ 11 REB ✅ 6 AST ✅ 7 STL (franchise record) He's only the second rookie to record at least 20p/10r/5a/5s in a playoff game since the NBA began tracking steals in 1973-74, joining Magic Johnson. Read, share, subscribe ⬇️ open.substack.com/pub/statitud...

item.title: Billy Donovan has an 'excellent shot' of landing Magic head coaching job
item.description: While I always hesitate to frame any potential hire as the favorite for a job, since NBA matters can be so fluid, coaching insiders across the league's landscape are very much operating under the belief that former Bulls coach Billy Donovan has an excellent shot at landing the post. Sources confirmed that Donovan is in dialogue with Magic president Jeff Weltman and will have an in-person meeting with Orlando's lead executive in the near future…Orlando has LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gun

item.title: Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bulls, Magic and Trail Blazers are all slated to make formal interview requests for all three franchises’ respective coaching vacancies this week, sources say.

item.title: Franz Wagner, Bogdan Bogdanović and VJ Edgecombe are …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Franz Wagner, Bogdan Bogdanović and VJ Edgecombe are among the players who will be special guest mentors at adidas Eurocamp 2026 on June 5-7 in Treviso, Italy. The following teams and athletes will participate:

item.title: Sources say Tom Thibodeau, to the contrary, is not …
item.description: Sources say Tom Thibodeau, to the contrary, is not involved in the Magic's interview process at this point. Yet it's well-established that Orlando wants to hire a coach with experience and sources tell both Marc Stein and me that Orlando has LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy on its radar as well. The defensive guru on Tyronn Lue's staff is the brother, of course, of former Magic coach Stan Van Gundy.

item.title: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is …
item.description: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is another candidate expected to interview with the Magic, sources say, even though Sweeney is seeking his first head coaching post. Sweeney's first formal conversation with Orlando might have to be conducted remotely given Sweeney's Western Conference finals duties with San Antonio. League sources say Sweeney is also a prime candidate for Chicago's opening created by Donovan's departure.

item.title: League sources say Orlando had serious interest in …
item.description: League sources say Orlando had serious interest in Kidd in the summer of 2021 when it last had a coaching vacancy and the same decision-makers (Jeff Weltman and top adviser John Hammond) remain in place.

item.title: "When Jamahl Mosley became available, I was told that …
item.description: Chris Mannix: "When Jamahl Mosley became available, I was told that the Pelicans shifted their interest quickly and immediately to Jamahl Mosley. Jamahl has a history of taking over young developing teams and turning them into winners. His first year in Orlando, the Magic won 22 games, they won 47 two years later. Now, they were never really able to build on that to become the kind of sustainable contender that Orlando fans, the Orlando organization had hoped for."

item.title: Josh Hart before Game 1: 'These 20-point leads right now at this point in the season, they literally mean nothing'
item.description: Josh Hart: "And I think with this year, you're seeing these 20-point leads dwindle fast. I mean, how many times is Detroit came back in Orlando being down 20 a couple games. You've seen Philly's done it, Minnesota just did it last night and then they erased like a 20-point lead, then got tied and then gave up, you know, gave up and lost by like 20 again. But these 20-point leads right now at this point in the season, they literally mean nothing. So that's why you have to continue that, you know,

item.title: But when I look at the Trail Blazers roster, that …
item.description: Draymond Green: "Jason Kidd from Oakland. Damian Lillard also from Oakland. Point guard. You look at Scoot Henderson, a young guard. How can Jason Kidd help him? So when you take a step back and you look at those jobs, I definitely can see Jason Kidd taking the Trail Blazers job. Now, however, it's been widely known that the owner of their team doesn't want to pay the head coach. It's been widely publicized. A lot of people have been talking about it. And quite honestly, you're making $10 millio

item.title: Sean Sweeney is expected to generate …
item.description: Once the Spurs’ season concludes, Sean Sweeney is expected to generate considerable interest in the coaching market. The Chicago Bulls and Orlando Magic are two other teams that could try to bring him in for an interview.

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy …
item.description: This week, the Magic interviewed at least one candidate for the job that opened May 4 — the day Magic president of basketball operations Jeff Weltman dismissed Jamahl Mosley. Los Angeles Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy interviewed for the position, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel on Friday.

item.title: Jamahl Mosley’s coaching staff is expected to include …
item.description: Jamahl Mosley’s coaching staff is expected to include Pelicans holdovers Greg Monroe, Jodie Meeks, Mike Hopkins, and Will Bynum, league sources told HoopsHype. Additionally, Dale Osbourne and Bret Brielmaier, who were both a part of Mosley’s assistant coaching staff with the Orlando Magic, are strong candidates to join Mosley in New Orleans, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Another fan paid $1,505 for Max Strus’ city edition …
item.description: The Cavs and the Realest also auctioned off a Lakers bench seat from their game on Jan. 28 for $1,905. A game-used rim and net from that same game sold for $2,305. Another fan paid $1,505 for Max Strus’ city edition game-worn warmup pullover ahead of the Cavs’ game against the Orlando Magic on March 24. And yet… “We don’t want to sell only $10,000 items,” Keeney said. “That’s not accessible for the kid that’s growing up. We need to onboard a new generation and demographic of collectors.” Hence,

item.title: Charlie Villanueva doesn't have Shai Gilgeous-Alexander in his Top 10 point guards ever
item.description: Charlie Villanueva on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: I wouldn't even put him in the top 10. Q. Give me 10 point guards, combo point guards, it doesn’t matter. Villanueva: Magic Johnson, Isiah Thomas, Stephen Curry, Allen Iverson, John Stockton… I wouldn't put him up past John Stockton, fam. Q. But that's only five you've named so far. Villanueva: Okay, let me get there! Steve Nash, Chris Paul… Okay. I'm cool. I'm cool with all these… Oh, also Tony Parker. (…) He’s not in front of Jason Kidd. (…) Bob C

item.title: When you go into your next job after having the …
item.description: What was your message to him when he was fired by the Magic? JB Bickerstaff: We talk about everything all the time. I told him that his next job was going to be his best job. When you get a job as a first-time coach, you’re kind of at the organization’s whim because they have to choose you. When you go into your next job after having the success that he had, you get an opportunity to choose. You get an opportunity to ask questions that first-time head coaches don’t get to ask. It’s important tha

item.title: His willingness to sacrifice of himself for the …
item.description: What makes Mosley a good coach? JB Bickerstaff: The way that he cares about the people he works with. His willingness to sacrifice of himself for the greater good and for the players he’s coaching and the organization as a whole. I think that’s where it starts. But then you look at his teams and his teams are always well coached. Defensively, they are always really good, really sound. Offensively, they had a plan. You had to game-plan to beat his teams. His teams didn’t beat themselves.

item.title: Former #Magic coach Jamahl Mosley shouted out God …
item.description: Jason Beede: Former #Magic coach Jamahl Mosley shouted out God Shammgod, Bret Brielmaier, Dale Osbourne, and Randy Gregory as members of his coaching staff in attendance for his introductory press conference with the Pelicans in New Orleans.

item.title: Mosley is bringing over four assistants he worked with …
item.description: Mosley is bringing over four assistants he worked with in Orlando. Bret Brielmaier will be his offensive coordinator, Dale Osbourne his defensive coordinator. Randy Gregory will serve as head of player development, while God Shammgod will play a hybrid role as a player development coach and a voice Mosley trusts on in-game adjustments.

item.title: Magic seriously interested in Sean Sweeney as new head coach
item.description: League sources have conveyed with increasing volume this week that Orlando's interest in San Antonio's Sean Sweeney to fill its coaching vacancy is more serious than previously known. "They're very interested," said one league coaching source. The Magic's plan, league sources say, was to travel to San Antonio during the Spurs' Western Conference finals showdown with the Thunder to meet face-to-face with Sweeney while he is engaged in an active playoff run.

item.title: Magic to hire Sean Sweeney as head coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic will hire Sean Sweeney as their head coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Sweeney was an associate head coach for the San Antonio Spurs. Prior, he was an assistant coach for the Dallas Mavericks, Detroit Pistons, Milwaukee Bucks, and Brooklyn Nets.

item.title: Sweeney will finish the remainder of the postseason on …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sweeney will finish the remainder of the postseason on the Spurs bench -- with Game 7 on Saturday night for an NBA Finals berth. Former Chicago Bulls head coach Billy Donovan and LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy were the two other finalists for the Magic's coaching vacancy, sources said.

item.title: Orlando a team to watch for Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: NBA insider Ramona Shelburne appeared on the "Rich Eisen Show" on Friday, suggesting that the Magic might be looking to make a trade for Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo. "I would look at a team like Orlando," Shelburne said. "They're close, they're already making a move, they're going to get a new coach who has some good young pieces."

item.title: Vassell on Sweeney getting Orlando job: "Definitely …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Vassell on Sweeney getting Orlando job: "Definitely happy for him. The impact he's had here has been tremendous, challenging everybody to be better on defensive end, holding us to a certain standard. I wish him all the luck, but right now we're focused on him being here now."

item.title: Special guests Franz Wagner (Orlando Magic) and …
item.description: Special guests Franz Wagner (Orlando Magic) and Eurocamp alumni Bogdan Bogdanović (Los Angeles Clippers) and VJ Edgecombe (Philadelphia 76ers) will share their experiences and personal paths to the NBA with the next generation of global talent.

item.title: “He’s had many opportunities of potentially becoming a …
item.description: “Thrilled for Sean. He deserves it,” Johnson said Saturday before the Spurs played the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference finals. “He’s had many opportunities of potentially becoming a head coach and so it was a matter of when, not if, even when I reached out to him to speak with him for the first time.”

item.title: I mean, he’s challenged everybody to be better on the …
item.description: “Definitely happy for him,” Spurs guard Devin Vassell said. “The impact that he’s had here has been tremendous. I mean, he’s challenged everybody to be better on the defensive end. He’s holding us to a certain standard. You know, I wish him all the luck.”

item.title: The 19th edition of the adidas Eurocamp will take …
item.description: The 19th edition of the adidas Eurocamp will take place from June 5–7 in Treviso, serving as a premier pre-draft showcase for international basketball players aged 16 to 22. This year’s event features a high-profile lineup of special guests, highlighted by Orlando Magic forward Franz Wagner and Eurocamp alumnus Bogdan Bogdanovic. The German and Serbian players will be joined by fellow NBA star VJ Edgecombe to mentor the prospects gathered in Italy. Besides the active NBA players, the rosters for

item.title: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next …
item.description: ‪Jason Beede‬: New: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next head coach, the #Magic announced. Sweeney will stick with the Spurs through the Finals. “Sean brings a tremendous work ethic and a high degree of intensity that set the tone for everything he does,” Jeff Weltman said.

item.title: He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that …
item.description: Sweeney, 41, will continue with the Spurs through the NBA Finals, the Magic said. San Antonio has homecourt advantage when it takes on the New York Knicks in the Finals, which start Wednesday night on ABC. He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that includes an additional one-year team option, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Sports Illustrated first reported the length of Sweeney’s contract.

item.title: Magic hosted a predraft workout today with the …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Source: The Magic hosted a predraft workout today with the following prospects: Rashaun Agee, Texas A&M. Tyler Bilodeau, UCLA. Chaze Harris, South Alabama. Rienk Mast, Nebraska. Kowacie Reeves Jr., Georgia Tech. Malik Reneau, Miami (FL).

item.title: Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts …
item.description: Tobias Bass: Miami’s Tre Donaldson has completed NBA pre-draft workouts with the Jazz, Suns, Spurs, Lakers, Warriors, Clippers, Knicks He will also workout with the Hornets, Bucks, Heat, Kings, Raptors, Celtics, Pistons, Magic, Nuggets and Trail Blazers

item.title: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to …
item.description: NBA Communications: The Knicks are the third team in NBA Finals history to win the first two games of a series on the road: 1993 — Bulls (vs. Suns); 1995 — Rockets (vs. Magic); 2026 — Knicks (vs. Spurs)

item.title: The Magic hosted a predraft workout today that …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Source: The Magic hosted a predraft workout today that included the following players: 🏀 Kylan Boswell, Illinois 🏀 BJ Edwards, SMU 🏀 Nate Johnson, Kansas State 🏀 Tobi Lawal, Virginia Tech

item.title: The Brooklyn Nets and the Milwaukee Bucks have …
item.description: James Barlowe: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets and the Milwaukee Bucks have recently both visited Mikel Brown Jr in Orlando to watch workouts and meet with the family. Brown Jr is scheduled to visit the Atlanta Hawks, Brooklyn Nets, Los Angeles Clippers and Dallas Mavericks before the draft.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama with 32 points in the win, becoming …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Victor Wembanyama with 32 points in the win, becoming the third-youngest player to have 30+ points in the Finals in NBA history. Youngest since Magic Johnson in 1980.

item.title: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of …
item.description: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of the headlines, as the projected No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft, Keba Keita has consistently impressed NBA teams throughout the predraft process. “Keba is on the rise,” his agent, Keith Kreiter, said. “He’s been outplaying bigs who are projected to go in the first round. His physicality and ability to guard one through five makes him a no-brainer for the mid-to-late second round.” He’s worked out with the Utah Jazz, Washington Wizards, Charlo

item.title: Tristan Thompson: Stephen Curry is the GOAT of point guards
item.description: Tristan Thompson: Steph Curry is the best point guard ever played. Richard Jefferson: For me, it's Magic Johnson and then Steph. Thompson: Oh, yeah. Cuz that's your age bracket.

item.title: No significant recent trade talks between Magic and Bucks on Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Minnesota and Golden State were both prominent Giannis Antetokounmpo suitors in February when the Bucks briefly invited trade interest in their Face of the Franchise, but sources say neither team has factored into the Bucks' recent trade talks. I also continued to hear that there hasn't been any significant recent dialogue between the Magic and Bucks.

item.title: Per league and team sources, the Bucks appear to be …
item.description: Per league and team sources, the Bucks appear to be seeking clarity from several suitors as to what they would actually be willing to offer before making a final decision. The Boston Celtics, Minnesota Timberwolves and Orlando Magic, the sources say, are known to be among them. The Portland Trail Blazers are known to have interest in trading for Antetokounmpo, but it appears more likely — if the Bucks have their way — that they would be involved as a third-team as a way for Milwaukee to regain c

item.title: You're hearing Kawhi Leonard. You're hearing maybe a …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: If I were to handicap all of the possibilities out there, I do think that Giannis is a really hard one to pull off. I think Kyrie is a more realistic possibility. You are hearing some other names start to just get kind of thrown around the rumor mill. You're hearing Kawhi Leonard. You're hearing maybe a little bit of Jalen Suggs, a few other names.

item.title: Franz Wagner on playing in Berlin game: I probably shouldn't have played, but I don't really regret the decision to play
item.description: Injuries hit you hard this past year. How tough was that mentally and physically? Looking back, do you regret playing in the Berlin game, or did it worsen the injury? Franz Wagner: It was very challenging. At the end of the day, if we're not healthy as athletes, we can't do our jobs, so that's the most important thing. And so there's always that pressure that you need to be out there and perform and help the team out. So not being able to do that and not seeing the results in rehab, too, when yo

item.title: And every great team has gone through disappointments …
item.description: After consecutive premature playoff exits, what lessons are you taking from these past few years? Are there specific routines you're building or changing to help get the team to the next level? Franz Wagner: The hope is that you learn these lessons once, and then you've got it down the next year. I can just say, we got a really talented roster. We got guys with high character and dudes that want to work hard and get better. And that's a really good starting point. And every great team has gone t

item.title: Heat will play preseason game in Orlando on Oct. 16
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Heat will play preseason game in Orlando on Oct. 16.

item.title: New Magic coach Sean Sweeney will make his preseason …
item.description: New Magic coach Sean Sweeney will make his preseason debut inside Kia Center on Oct. 13 when Orlando hosts the Cleveland Cavaliers. The Magic announced Monday morning they will play two preseason home games in 2026. Orlando will also host the Miami Heat on Oct. 16, the team said. Both home preseason games will start at 7 p.m., the Magic said.

item.title: Hornets have Nic Claxton on their radar
item.description: With Charlotte considering a starting-caliber center upgrade, other centers besides Sabonis are on their radar, including Brooklyn Nets center Nic Claxton, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, it’s worth noting that the Nets have expressed interest in Hornets free agent guard Coby White in the past when he was on the Chicago Bulls, sources said. In other Hornets news, assistant coach Zach Peterson is a candidate to become the franchise’s next head coach of their G League affiliate, the Gr

item.title: Orlando Magic will add Riley Crean as an assistant …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Orlando Magic will add Riley Crean as an assistant coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Crean was the head video coordinator of the Dallas Mavericks and spent three of four seasons there working with Orlando Magic coach Sean Sweeney. Riley, 27, is the son of coach Tom Crean

item.title: Orlando Magic will add DJ Bakker as an assistant …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Orlando Magic will add DJ Bakker as an assistant coach. He was a Hornets assistant coach and head coach of the Greensboro Swarm in the G League. He’s been an assistant for the Bucks and Pistons. Bakker and Orlando Magic coach Sean Sweeney worked together in Detroit.

item.title: Orlando Magic will add Mfon Udofia as an assistant …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Orlando Magic will add Mfon Udofia as an assistant coach. He won the most games in Long Island Nets history as head coach and had 3 NBA call-ups this year. He was Nigeria’s head coach at the FIBA AfroBasket 2021 Qualifiers and an assistant at the 2019 FIBA World Cup.

item.title: I've since been told that a Milwaukee contingent …
item.description: For now, though, let's say that the Bucks do stay at No. 10. We've already written about Milwaukee's interest in the top guards on the board … everyone from Darius Acuff Jr. to Keaton Wagler to Mikel Brown Jr. after flying down to Orlando to meet with Brown. I've since been told that a Milwaukee contingent including general manager Jon Horst and new head coach Taylor Jenkins also traveled to New York recently to visit with Nate Ament during this pre-draft process. Now the Bucks are being looked

item.title: #Magic players I’ve seen in attendance for coach Sean …
item.description: Jason Beede: #Magic players I’ve seen in attendance for coach Sean Sweeney’s introductory press conference are Jamal Cain and Jonathan Isaac as well as two-way players Colin Castleton and Alex Morales.

item.title: “The Magic were the most thorough and they did the …
item.description: Sean Sweeney landed interviews for more than a few NBA head coaching vacancies over the last few years, and many of them seemed the same. Talking to Orlando, he said, was different. Sweeney was introduced Thursday as the new coach of the Magic, which was the obvious proof that he won them over by his answers during the interview process. Turns out, the Magic won him over with their questions as well. “The Magic were the most thorough and they did the best job of asking questions and asking follo

item.title: “It was during the playoffs and not only was he able …
item.description: He stayed locked in on his tasks with the Spurs, even though the interview process started early in their postseason run. But with the finals over, Sweeney wasted little time in getting to Orlando and starting on the new job. “When we finally did get in front of him, it was a long day and he had a lot going on,” Magic President Jeff Weltman said. “It was during the playoffs and not only was he able to kind of compartmentalize his work with the Spurs and present well to us, but he really knocked

item.title: “Coach Pop’s office was right next to mine and I was …
item.description: “Coach Pop’s office was right next to mine and I was very fortunate,” Sweeney said. “When you’re next to a guy who’s the greatest coach of all time daily, the fact that he even said hello and knew my name meant something to me. So yeah, Coach Pop, he built that program and guys like me are fortunate to be part of it. I’m very grateful and that’s part of the reason why I’m here now.”

item.title: When he was born, I sat out a year of college …
item.description: At what point did you know your son could be an NBA player? Brown Sr.: When he was born, I sat out a year of college basketball. So, I had my senior year left. When I went back to play, Kel used to run up and down the sidelines all the time. So, I remember we were playing at Valdosta State. I ended up playing at West Florida. And my pops said, ‘Son, before doing warm-ups, he’s moving around, he’s playing around the whole time.’ He said, ‘But as soon as that ball is tipped, he froze and just stop

item.title: Jeff Weltman on Giannis Antetokounmpo talk: To be thrown into that conversation speaks to the talent of the Magic roster
item.description: Jeff Weltman was asked by the Orlando Sentinel about the idea that the Magic are a “dark horse” team to land Giannis Antetokounmpo. “First of all, you know I’m not allowed to speak about that,” Weltman told the Sentinel on Thursday after Sweeney was formally introduced at the AdventHealth Training Center. “I’m not allowed to talk about another team’s player.” “I just think that, to me, “Weltman said, “I take it as a compliment that we have a lot of players that the league is interested in and th

item.title: “I know a lot’s been written about that. But I think, …
item.description: “Obviously I won’t comment on any discussions we’re having with teams or where our agendas are,” Weltman told the Sentinel. “I know a lot’s been written about that. But I think, like, you can’t get into that conversation without talent, you know? So, to me, I take that as a compliment and I know we have a lot of work to do, but we have a lot of belief in our team.”

item.title: “We’re doing all the work that we need to (do) to try …
item.description: “We’re doing all the work that we need to (do) to try to puzzle the roster out,” Weltman said Thursday. “I do think there’s some kind of basic holes that we want to punch where we can improve the roster and augment the players that we have. “Obviously the most important part of this whole process has been bringing Sean in the right way,” he added. “… It’s been a busy time organizationally but we have been at work on the draft and free agency, so hopefully we’re putting ourselves in a position to

item.title: He had already elbowed me in my sternum and knocked …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Matt Barnes on when he fake threw the ball in Kobe Bryant face in the finals: “The Orlando sh*t I told him in our last interview like bro I was ready to fu*king like I was ready to go, like fu*k basketball. Was ready to throw that ball in your face. Because he gets like he’s already so good but then he’ll elbow you and grab you and I remember I caught a dunk tip off the rim because he was guarding me and he tried to block Dwight shot and it fell of perfect and I put that b***h dow

item.title: NBA execs believe Magic will eventually choose between Jalen Suggs and Anthony Black
item.description: While the contract of Isaac gives Orlando flexibility to shed salary or use as a potential trade chip, the immediacy of Black’s extension talks will be interesting following Jalen Suggs getting a five-year, $150 million extension, and with Black having comparable statistics to Suggs last season. NBA executives across the league believe Orlando will eventually have to choose between keeping Suggs or Black on the roster long-term due to their incoming salary cap situation.

item.title: Sources say other assistant coaching candidates in the …
item.description: Meanwhile, Sean Sweeney’s coaching staff has begun to take shape with HoopsHype reporting the additions of DJ Bakker, Mfon Udofia, and Riley Crean as assistant coaches. Sources say other assistant coaching candidates in the mix for Sweeney’s staff include Joe Prunty, Popeye Jones, Greg Buckner, and Tom Bialaszewski.

item.title: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences …
item.description: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences getting to know fresh faces in the Fiji setting — and the astute might recognize one name. Aniya Harvey is the daughter of retired NBA star Donnell Harvey, who played for the Dallas Mavericks, Orlando Magic, New Jersey Nets, Denver Nuggets and Phoenix Suns. Harvey made his final NBA appearance in 2005 but continued to play professionally internationally until 2014. According to Complex (1), Harvey has largely been out of the spotlight since he

item.title: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. …
item.description: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. That’s 150 first-round picks. First question: did they make an impact for more than a month or two for the team that drafted them? The obvious choices are Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball (2020); Cade Cunningham (2021) and Stephon Castle (2024). Throw in guys like Onyeka Okongwu (2020) and Anthony Black (2023) who clearly are difference makers for the teams that drafted them or traded for them on draft night. How many of that 150 total made t

item.title: Milwaukee is acquiring No. 60 from the Magic for cash,
item.description: Shams Charania: Milwaukee is acquiring No. 60 from the Magic for cash, sources said.

item.title: Magic trade No. 46 pick Felix Okpara to Wizards
item.description: Michael Scotto: TRADE: The Washington Wizards are acquiring Felix Okpara, the No. 46 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, from the Orlando Magic in exchange for the No. 51 and 60 picks, per league sources.

item.title: Orlando traded No. 46 Felix Okpara to Washington for …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: Orlando traded No. 46 Felix Okpara to Washington for Nos. 51 and 60.

item.title: Magic draft Felix Okpara at No. 46
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Orlando Magic will select Felix Okpara with the No. 46 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Magic selects Izaiyah Nelson at No. 51
item.description: Jake Fischer: Izaiyah Nelson will be the pick at No. 51, sources say.

item.title: Orlando Magic will select Izaiyah Nelson with the 51st …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Orlando Magic will select Izaiyah Nelson with the 51st pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Orlando used the No. 51 pick to select Nelson, the …
item.description: After remaining out of the first round of the NBA draft on Tuesday night, the Magic made multiple trades throughout the second round on Wednesday before ultimately landing on South Florida forward Izaiyah Nelson. Entering the night with only the No. 46 pick, Orlando sent that pick, which was used to select Tennessee forward Felix Okpara, to the Washington Wizards in exchange for the Nos. 51 and 60 draft picks, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Shortly after, the Magic then sent

item.title: Malik Reneau has signed a deal with the Orlando Magic,
item.description: Vince Wolfram: Miami’s Malik Reneau has signed a deal with the Orlando Magic, per ADS Sports Management. The 2025–26 All-ACC First Team selection, who led the Hurricanes in scoring at 18.9 PPG last season, is headed to Orlando.

item.title: Moritz Wagner projects to have multiple suitors in …
item.description: Sources say Moritz Wagner projects to have multiple suitors in free agency and a willingness to play elsewhere even though his brother Franz obviously remains a Magic cornerstone.

item.title: Jonathan Isaac waived by Magic
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic has waived Jonathan Isaac, league sources told @hoopshype. The former sixth overall pick of the 2017 NBA Draft averaged 6.8 points and 4.5 rebounds in seven seasons with the Magic.

item.title: Magic to save $6.5 million in 2026-27 salaries after waiving Jonathan Isaac
item.description: Michael Scotto: Jonathan Isaac was guaranteed $8 million of his $14.5 million salary for the 2026-27 season. The following two seasons on his deal were non-guaranteed. The 28-year-old, 6-foot-10 forward now becomes an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: While Isaac could still re-sign with Orlando, he will …
item.description: With the move, the Magic give themselves some financial flexibility by creating more space below the second apron and lowering their projected luxury payment from roughly $16.5 million down to around $10 million for the upcoming season. The franchise is expected to pay luxury tax for the first time in over a decade. While Isaac could still re-sign with Orlando, he will also be allowed to negotiate with all other clubs as a free agent starting at 6 p.m. on Tuesday. Teams may begin signing free ag

item.title: Pelicans, Rockets, Magic not in the mix for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Other teams, such as the New Orleans Pelicans, Houston Rockets, and Orlando Magic, who’ve been loosely linked to Brown, have been described as not in the mix.

item.title: Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from Lakers, Celtics, Heat, Clippers, Magic
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Nuggets navigate getting below the second apron, sharpshooting forward Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Los Angeles Lakers, Boston Celtics, Miami Heat, Los Angeles Clippers, and Orlando Magic, league sources told HoopsHype. The 30-year-old forward shot career-highs from the field (.480) and beyond the arc (.430), and is on an expiring $23.06 million contract for the upcoming season.

item.title: Spurs, Magic and Sixers interested in John Collins
item.description: League sources say John Collins certainly has fans in Minnesota, but it appears unlikely that the Timberwolves will have sufficient spending power to pursue the scoring forward, who spent last season with the Clippers. Collins is also expected to draw interest from Orlando, San Antonio and Philadelphia once free agency begins. The 76ers and Western Conference champion Spurs continue to get linked in free agency projections to the top forwards in the marketplace.

item.title: Clippers exploring re-signing John Collins amid interest from Spurs, Magic, and 76ers
item.description: We're also hearing that the Clippers might elect to try to re-sign forward John Collins, who as The Stein Line previously reported is expected to draw interest from the likes of San Antonio, Orlando and Philadelphia once free agency formally begins Tuesday night at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Clippers interested in Goga Bitadze
item.description: Word is that the Clippers are also among the teams that have expressed trade interest in Orlando's Goga Bitadze. The Stein Line has since learned, though, that the Magic have rebuffed inquires for the Georgian big man to this point. It appears that Orlando wants to retain Bitadze in part because it is bracing for the loss of Moritz Wagner, brother of Magic star Franz Wagner, in unrestricted free agency.

item.title: We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Two trades that we’re monitoring today… The Clippers and Raptors’continuing conversations on Kawhi Leonard, plus the Hawks working on a big addition. We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks fans, as the Magic aren’t talking trade for their reserve center, sources say, while anticipating Mo Wagner will leave Orlando in free agency.

item.title: #Magic have given Colin Castleton a two-way qualifying …
item.description: Jason Beede: The #Magic have given Colin Castleton a two-way qualifying offer, a league source told the @orlandosentinel. He’ll be a restricted free agent.

item.title: Moritz Wagner on Nets' radar
item.description: They were reportedly interested in Mitchell Robinson but the Nets also are expected to show strong interest in Orlando’s Mo Wagner in free agency, per sources. Brooklyn obviously wouldn’t sign both Robinson and Wagner.

item.title: Nikola Vucevic agrees to one-year deal to return to Orlando
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Nikola Vucevic has agreed on a one-year, $3.9 million deal to return to the Orlando Magic, sources tell ESPN. Vucevic -- who started his career in Orlando as a sophomore, spent nearly nine years with the Magic and has maintained a home there since -- turned down more lucrative offers elsewhere to reunite with the franchise.

item.title: The Magic are likely limited to minimum signings this …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Magic are likely limited to minimum signings this offseason due to their tax and apron constraints. Not much better they can do bargain-hunting than Nikola Vucevic back on a minimum salary.

item.title: Nets, Moritz Wagner agree to two-year deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: Breaking: Moritz Wagner is signing a two-year, $19 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources say. The deal is fully guaranteed and includes a second-year mutual option. He gets paid nearly double his salary from last season after spending six seasons with the Orlando Magic

item.title: He told me three years ago he wanted his family to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Very cool for Nikola Vucevic to go back to the Magic. He told me three years ago he wanted his family to live in Orlando upon retirement. And we know the Magic are expecting Moritz Wagner to depart the franchise this week, likely to Brooklyn.

item.title: Jevon Carter to return to Magic on one-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Jevon Carter has agreed to a one-year, $3.5 million deal to stay with the Orlando Magic, sources tell ESPN. Carter finished the season in Orlando and earned a rotation role.

item.title: I've got the Magic about $6.2M under the second apron …
item.description: Keith Smith: Jevon Carter is getting a one-year deal for the veteran minimum. He'll hit the cap at roughly $2.4M. Same cap hit as Nikola Vucevic. I've got the Magic about $6.2M under the second apron with two open roster spots. Could squeeze in a Taxpayer MLE signing, but likely won't to avoid a second-apron hard cap.

item.title: Magic, Jonathan Isaac agree to one-year contract
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic and Jonathan Isaac have agreed to a one-year deal, league sources told @hoopshype .

item.title: Colin Castle agrees to two-way with Orlando
item.description: Dushawn London: Colin Castleton has agreed to a two-way contract with the Orlando Magic, source tell @247Sports .

item.title: Magic sign Izaiyah Nelson to two-way
item.description: The Magic signed second-round pick Izaiyah Nelson to a two-way contract, the team announced Wednesday night.

item.title: After signing Nikola Vucevic, Jonathan Isaac and Jevon …
item.description: After signing Nikola Vucevic, Jonathan Isaac and Jevon Carter earlier Wednesday to one-year veteran minimum deals, the Magic sit roughly $2.5 million under the second apron, a salary threshold set at $221,686,000 by the collective bargaining agreement.

item.title: Former Top 10 pick Cam Reddish to play Summer League with Orlando Magic
item.description: Brett Siegel: Former 10th overall pick in 2019 and six-year veteran wing Cam Reddish will be participating in NBA Summer League with the Orlando Magic.

item.title: 2 staffers/coaches headed to Orlando w/Sweeney.
item.description: Michael C. Wright: 2 staffers/coaches headed to Orlando w/Sweeney. 2 more are headed for college gigs at Baylor and Pepperdine.

item.title: Izaiyah Nelson signed a two-year, two-way contract …
item.description: 2026 second-round pick Izaiyah Nelson signed a two-year, two-way contract with the Orlando Magic.

item.title: Jevon Carter waived his implied no-trade clause in his …
item.description: Keith Smith: Jevon Carter waived his implied no-trade clause in his new deal with the Orlando Magic, a league source told @spotrac.

item.title: Izaiyah Nelson's two-way contract with the Orlando …
item.description: Keith Smith: Izaiyah Nelson's two-way contract with the Orlando Magic is for two seasons, a league source told @spotrac.

item.title: The arena rises to its feet as a tearful Nikola …
item.description: BasketNews: The arena rises to its feet as a tearful Nikola Vucevic is honored following his national team retirement 🥺🇲🇪 #FIBAWC #StepItUp

item.title: Taberner has done stints at Everton, the Orlando …
item.description: Shamit Dua: Taberner has done stints at Everton, the Orlando Magic, and Manchester City (Academy). Taberner has a doctorate in applied sport and exercise science from Liverpool John Moores University. Like David Cosgrave, extensive experience in professional soccer

item.title: The Orlando Magic hired Joe Prunty, Greg Buckner, …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Orlando Magic hired Joe Prunty, Greg Buckner, Popeye Jones, Tom Bialaszewski, Riley Crean, Jon Harris, Ben Johnson, Jacqlyn Poss and Mfon Udofia as assistant coaches. D.J. Bakker is assistant coach/Director of PD. Shannan Lum is assistant coach/Director of coaching operations

item.title: The Washington Wizards and Felix Okpara have agreed to …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Washington Wizards and Felix Okpara have agreed to a two-way contract, league sources told @hoopshype . Okpara was the 46th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and was an SEC All-Defensive Team selection. He was acquired by the Wizards from the Orlando Magic in a trade on draft night.

item.title: They've been great. They've been great. Me and him …
item.description: How have some of the early conversations with Sweeney been? Two thinkers of the game getting together. Paolo Banchero: They've been great. They've been great. Me and him have really hit it off so far. He came out to Seattle last week, worked out with me for a couple days. And obviously we've been working out here in Vegas with the rest of the coaching staff. I'm just really excited about what he can do for me and the team. Like you said, he's a great mind on both sides of the ball. Extremely det

item.title: We got a chance to win the East
item.description: In terms of the balance of power in the NBA, it certainly feels like things have shifted toward the East. Have you felt something similar? Paolo Banchero: I mean it's a lot of big names coming to the East. I've always felt like the East is the better conference, even though I think in the past we've been more slept on. I think you saw that this year with the Knicks and their run. Guys see it around the league. The East is wide open in my opinion — a lot of guys feel that, that's why a lot of fre
